site.name: Wren & Willow Hair Studio

footer.blurb: A six-chair hair studio on Edmonton's 124 Street — cuts, colour, and honest advice since 2014.

meta.title: Wren & Willow Hair Studio — Cuts, Colour & Balayage in Edmonton
meta.description: A six-chair hair studio on 124 Street. Precision cuts, lived-in colour, and stylists who listen first. Book online or drop us a line.

fields.headline: Hair that still looks good three weeks from now.
fields.subhead: We're a six-chair studio that books real consultation time, formulates colour to grow out gracefully, and never rushes a haircut to fit a schedule.

fields.heading: A studio, not a conveyor belt
body: Wren & Willow opened in 2014 with one belief: the difference between a fine haircut and a great one is the twenty minutes nobody else budgets for. Every appointment here starts in the chair, talking — about your hair's history, how much time you actually spend on it in the morning, and what "low maintenance" really means to you.
Six stylists, one standard. Whether you're in for a fringe trim or a full colour correction, you'll leave knowing exactly what we did, why we did it, and how to keep it looking like day one.

cards.title: Cuts & Styling
cards.body: Precision cuts built around your hair's natural fall — so it works on the days you don't style it, too.

cards.title: Colour & Balayage
cards.body: Lived-in colour formulated to grow out softly. No hard lines, no panic at week five.

cards.title: Treatments & Texture
cards.body: Bond repair, deep conditioning, and texture services that treat the hair you have, not the hair on the box.

pairs.title: Balayage refresh
pairs.caption: Grown-out box colour lifted into a soft, dimensional balayage — one session with Sofia.

pairs.title: Silver transition
pairs.caption: Eighteen months of dyed ends blended into natural silver, instead of one harsh chop.

quotes.quote: Maren is the first stylist who asked what my hair does on a Tuesday morning, not just what I wanted for the photo. Three years later I've never once dreaded a grow-out.

I came in with a colour-correction disaster from a box kit and left feeling like myself again. Sofia talked me out of the expensive option because it wasn't what my hair needed. That's why I trust them.
As someone with thick, wavy hair that every barber just thins into submission, finding Jules was a revelation. Best men's cut in the city, full stop.

fields.statement: Your next appointment is two clicks away.
fields.subtext: Browse real-time availability for every stylist, pick your time, and get a reminder the day before.
fields.note: New colour clients: please book a free 15-minute consultation first so we can plan your session length honestly.

meta.title: Services & Pricing | Wren & Willow Hair Studio
meta.description: Transparent pricing for cuts, balayage, vivids, and treatments. Every price is a starting point we confirm at consultation — no surprises at the till.

fields.heading: The service menu
fields.subhead: Every price below is a starting point we confirm together at consultation — never a surprise at the till.

fields.heading: Cutting services
fields.note: Longer or denser hair may add time; we'll always tell you before we start, not after.

plans.name: Signature Cut
plans.description: Our standard appointment — never under an hour.

plans.name: Fringe & Neck Trim
plans.description: Fifteen minutes to keep your shape sharp. Free if we cut your hair in the last six weeks.

plans.name: Blowout & Style
plans.description: Wash, blowout, and finish — for the days that matter.

plans.name: Event & Updo Styling
plans.description: Weddings, galas, graduations. Book a trial run two weeks ahead — we insist, and you'll be glad.

fields.heading: Colour services
fields.note: All colour services include a gloss and a bond-protecting additive. Colour corrections are quoted individually after a free consultation.

plans.name: Full Balayage
plans.description: Hand-painted, lived-in colour designed to grow out for months, not weeks.

plans.name: Root Touch-Up
plans.description: Colour to the line, matched to your existing shade.

plans.name: Vivid Colour
plans.description: Coppers, roses, blues — pigment-heavy work priced by session length.

plans.name: Gloss & Tone
plans.description: Twenty minutes to revive tone and shine between colour appointments.

steps.title: We talk first
steps.body: Every appointment starts in the chair with dry hair — how it behaves, what's been done to it, and how much morning effort you're honestly willing to spend.

steps.title: We agree the plan
steps.body: You'll hear exactly what we recommend, what it costs, and — for big colour changes — whether it needs more than one session. Then you decide.

steps.title: The work itself
steps.body: Unhurried, explained as we go. Colour clients get a card on file recording the exact formula, so a great result is repeatable.

steps.title: You leave with a plan
steps.body: A two-minute walkthrough of how to style it yourself, what to use, and when we should see you next. No pressure to buy a shelf of product.

items.question: Do you take walk-ins?
items.answer: When a chair is free, gladly — but most days are fully booked, so calling ahead is the safe bet. Fringe trims are the easiest to slot in.

items.question: Why do new colour clients need a consultation first?
items.answer: Because colour quoted sight-unseen is a guess, and we don't book guesses. Fifteen free minutes lets us assess your hair's history and book the right session length — which protects your time and your budget.

items.question: How long does balayage take?
items.answer: Plan for three to four hours for a full balayage, including toning and styling. Big transformations and corrections can run longer — we'll tell you at consultation, not on the day.

items.question: Do you cut children's hair?
items.answer: Yes — ages eight and up, weekday afternoons. Little kids do best with our quieter time slots, so mention their age when you book.

items.question: What's your cancellation policy?
items.answer: Life happens — give us 24 hours' notice and there's no charge, ever. Same-day cancellations and no-shows are charged 50% of the booked service, because that chair time can't be resold.

fields.statement: Not sure what to book? Ask us first.
fields.subtext: Send us a photo of your hair and what you're hoping for — we'll tell you what to book and how long it'll take.

meta.title: About the Studio | Wren & Willow Hair Studio
meta.description: Meet the six stylists behind Wren & Willow, the promises we keep, and our hours. A 124 Street studio built on consultation-first hairdressing since 2014.

fields.heading: The studio on 124 Street
fields.subhead: Twelve years, six chairs, and a stubborn belief that listening is half the haircut.

fields.heading: Started small on purpose, staying that way on purpose
body: Maren Holt opened Wren & Willow in 2014 after a decade in high-volume salons, where appointment slots got shorter every year and consultations disappeared entirely. The plan was simple and a little contrarian: fewer chairs, longer appointments, and prices honest enough to say out loud before the work starts.

Twelve years later the studio is still six chairs — by choice. Growth, for us, has meant deeper skills rather than more square footage: advanced colour certifications, curly-cutting training, and a colour-formula card for every regular so your best result is never a one-off.
You'll find us between the florist and the bookshop on 124 Street, in a bright corner space with plants we mostly manage to keep alive. Coffee's on; parking is free off the alley.

fields.heading: What we'll always do
items: Quote the real price before the cape goes on
Tell you when the cheaper service is the right one
Keep your colour formula on file, so results repeat
Start every appointment with a conversation, not a wash
Say "that won't suit your hair" when it's true
Never sell you product you don't need

members.name: Maren Holt
members.bio: Twenty-two years behind the chair. Maren cuts with the grow-out in mind and is the studio's last word on colour corrections.

members.name: Sofia Reyes
members.bio: Balayage and dimensional colour are Sofia's whole world. If your colour grew out gracefully, odds are she formulated it that way on purpose.

members.name: Jules Tran
members.bio: Curly, coily, and thick wavy hair — cut dry, shaped by hand. Jules trained in three curl methods so your hair doesn't have to fit one.

members.name: Priya Anand
members.bio: Bridal parties, galas, and hand-tied extensions. Priya is the reason we insist on updo trial runs — hers always survive the dance floor.

fields.heading: When to find us
fields.note: Evening appointments past 7pm are available Thursdays by request — ask when you book.

fields.statement: The best way to meet us is from the chair.
fields.subtext: First visit? Book a Signature Cut and we'll take it from there.
